nodes.label: Cloud Run Job for Simulation Bootstrap
NullPool for Celery (one connection per task)
Alert A1: Confidence Low (<0.6 for 5m, critical)
Alert A2: NO_DATA High (>25% for 5m, critical)
Alert A3: Rejection Rate High (>50% for 1h, warning)
Alert A4: Exchange Error Rate High (>10% for 5m, critical)
Grafana Scalpyn Trading Engine Monitoring README
Obsidian MOC Root Index (Scalpyn System Map)
Persistence Queue (Task #226, asyncio bounded queue + 4 workers)
5-Layer Scoring: Liquidity, Structure, Momentum, Volatility, Order Flow
3 Async DB Engines (pooled API, NullPool Celery, NullPool watchdog)
Leader Election — Single WS Writer Pattern
Celery Sentinel Queue __no_default__
nodes.rationale: Declared in task_queues to satisfy kombu (Celery ≥5.6 raises KeyError on send_task/beat if task_default_queue does not exist). Must not be removed.

nodes.label: acks_late Dual Policy (idempotent vs financial)
nodes.rationale: task_acks_late=True globally but idempotent beat-driven tasks override to acks_late=False (_NO_REQUEUE_ON_WORKER_LOSS). Financial execution tasks keep acks_late=True to avoid missed executions. Task #245.

nodes.label: HOSTNAME Cloud Run Fix (--hostname per worker)
nodes.rationale: HOSTNAME=localhost on every Cloud Run container causes all workers to announce as celery@localhost. inspect() deduplicates and reports Workers: 0 even with pipeline draining. start.sh generates a unique CELERY_NODENAME per container.

nodes.label: _run_async Pattern (Celery→asyncio bridge)
nodes.rationale: All Celery tasks are synchronous functions that internally use _run_async(coro) calling asyncio.new_event_loop() + loop.run_until_complete(coro). This requires NullPool sessions via run_db_task(fn, celery=True).

nodes.label: start.sh (entrypoint for all Cloud Run services)
Schema Readiness Gate (_SchemaReadinessGate middleware)
nodes.rationale: Allows uvicorn to bind immediately (Cloud Run probe passes), while alembic runs in background. Middleware returns 503 Retry-After=10 until /tmp/.migrations_done exists. Prevents Cloud Run from routing traffic to a container with incomplete schema.

nodes.label: procps Watchdog (zombie detection in start.sh)
nodes.rationale: start.sh:281 uses ps -o stat= -p for is_process_alive. Without procps package (not in python:3.12-slim), watchdog fails open and zombies go undetected. Must not remove procps from Dockerfile.

nodes.label: topology-check Build Step
nodes.rationale: Final step in cloudbuild.yaml that fails red if any of the 5 Cloud Run services is missing after deploy. Prevents silent pipeline stoppage from partial deploy. Must not be removed.

nodes.label: preflight-diagnostic Build Step
nodes.rationale: Logs identity, project, region, quota, IAM. Wrapped in || true (never fails). Enables debug of silent IAM/quota failures without re-running build.

nodes.label: Celery Probe Dual Budget (inspect timeout vs aggregate budget)
nodes.rationale: CELERY_INSPECT_TIMEOUT_S (per inspect call) and CELERY_INSPECT_BUDGET_S (asyncio.wait_for wrapper) must NOT be unified. In May 2026 they were equal (2s) and Redis Labs latency caused snapshot to report Workers: 0 with 5 live workers.

nodes.label: pool_starved ≠ ingestion_stale Alert Semantics
nodes.rationale: Empty pool suppresses ingestion_stale and emits pool_starved (severity info). Operator already knows empty pool = zero candles. Re-enabling stale alert for empty pool would be a false alarm.

nodes.label: Three-Queue Split Design Rationale
nodes.rationale: Before split, a single Celery queue caused two real incidents: (1) slow compute_indicators blocked evaluate_signals for full duration, causing late buys and missed force-close; (2) simulation burst of 30k items pushed pipeline_scan ticks 12 minutes late. Three queues give execution its own isolated workers.

nodes.label: Task Dedup Wrapper (SET NX EX via Redis)
nodes.rationale: All Celery enqueues inside app/tasks/ must go through task_dispatch.enqueue(). Wrapper uses Redis SET NX EX; duplicate dropped with DEDUP_SKIP log. Lock released by task_postrun signal. Fails open on Redis unreachable.

nodes.label: Queue Depth + Hysteresis Alert (10k/8k threshold)
nodes.rationale: Alert at depth>=10000 (ok→alerted), reset at depth<8000. Thresholds sized against simulation burst that defined worst-case queue length without false alerts during normal operation.

nodes.label: alembic stamp head Fallback Bug
nodes.rationale: When alembic upgrade head fails 3x (lock contention during rolling deploy), start.sh falls back to alembic stamp head. This writes the version to alembic_version but never executes the ALTER TABLE — column stays missing while ORM references it unconditionally.

nodes.label: WS Subscription vs Microstructure Scheduler Resolver Drift
nodes.rationale: Symbol approved in microstructure scheduler but not in WS subscription means trade buffer never fills — taker_ratio/volume_delta rely on REST fallback only. Root cause is pool_coin attached to futures pool but not a spot pool. Resolvers should share a helper in pool_service.

nodes.label: _safe_rollback Pattern (poison connection prevention)
nodes.rationale: Force best-effort rollback before connection returns to pool after any exception in run_db_task. Prevents InFailedSQLTransactionError / PendingRollbackError on next task picking up the same connection. Task #237.

nodes.label: Grafana Cloud Run Service (scalpyn-grafana)
nodes.rationale: Separate from main cloudbuild.yaml so dashboard JSON changes don't trigger backend redeploy. Uses public ingress (all) so browser can embed Grafana in iframe. Datasources reach DB over VPC connector private IP.

nodes.label: Symbol Format Convention (BTC_USDT underscore)
nodes.rationale: ALL symbols MUST be in BTC_USDT format with underscore. Normalization function: if _ not in s and s.endswith(USDT): return s[:-4] + _USDT. Applied in pools.py, watchlists.py, pipeline_scan.py, collect_market_data.py.

nodes.label: CeleryAsyncSessionLocal (NullPool for Celery)
nodes.rationale: Celery tasks must use CeleryAsyncSessionLocal (NullPool), not AsyncSessionLocal (pooled). NullPool is safe inside asyncio.run() / Celery worker event loops where a fresh event loop is created per task.
